heading: Claims for proceeds from sale of certain certificates: jurisdiction, limitations; divestment of copyrights: “copyrights” defined, rights of licensees and assignees, reproduction rights of United States, transfer of interests, payment of royalties to Attorney General, suits for infringement
label: 4339 Claims for proceeds from sale of certain certificates: jurisdiction, limitations; divestment of copyrights: “copyrights” defined, rights of licensees and assignees, reproduction rights of United States, transfer of interests, payment of royalties to Attorney General, suits for infringement

children.heading: Jurisdiction
children.label: a Jurisdiction
children.text: Notwithstanding any statute of limitation, lapse of time, any prior decision by any court of the United States, or any compromise, release or assignment to the Alien Property Custodian, jurisdiction is hereby conferred upon the United States Court of Federal Claims to hear, determine, and render judgment upon the claims against the United States for the proceeds received by the United States from the sale of the property vested under the provisions of this chapter by vesting order numbered 33 relating to certificate numbers 104 to 121, inclusive, 125, 126, 128 to 134, inclusive, and 137 to 139, inclusive. Proceedings with respect to such claims may be instituted hereunder not later than two years after .October 22, 1962

children.heading: “Copyrights” defined
children.label: b “Copyrights” defined
children.text: As used in this section the word “copyrights” includes copyrights, claims of copyrights, rights to copyrights, and rights to copyright renewals.

children.heading: Divestment of copyrights; limitations; transfer of remaining rights and interests
children.label: c Divestment of copyrights; limitations; transfer of remaining rights and interests

children.text: the rights of licensees under licenses issued by the Alien Property Custodian or the Attorney General in respect of such copyrights;
the rights of assignees under assignments by the Alien Property Custodian or the Attorney General of interests in such licenses; and
the right retained by the United States to reproduce, for its own use, or exhibit any divested copyrighted motion picture films.

children.heading: Rights or interests arising out of prevesting contracts
children.label: d Rights or interests arising out of prevesting contracts

children.text: royalties or other income received by or accrued in favor of the Alien Property Custodian or the Attorney General under such contracts;
rights or interests which have been returned or otherwise disposed of under this chapter; and
rights or interests vested by vesting orders 128 (7 F.R. 7578), 13111 (14 F.R. 1730), 14349 (15 F.R. 1575), and 17366 (16 F.R. 2483),

children.heading: Suits for infringement
children.label: e Suits for infringement
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to transfer to a person entitled to a copyright divested hereunder the right of the Attorney General to sue for the infringement of such copyright during the period between (1) the vesting thereof or the vesting of rights and interests in a contract entered into with respect thereto, and (2) the day of divestment. The right to sue for infringement shall remain in the Attorney General.
